title: Carbon Fund ETF Quarterly Trend Sheet - Q1 2025
content:
Carbon ETFs Face Volatility with NAV Declines, Fund Closures, and Signs of Recovery in Q1 2025

The carbon ETF market experienced significant fluctuations, with most funds seeing declines in NAV and AUM from Q1 2023 to Q1 2024. KRBN, a major carbon ETF, saw its NAV drop from $42.34 in Q1 2023 to $29.39 in Q1 2024, while AUM fell sharply from $712M to $295M, indicating reduced investor confidence. KCCA, however, saw a NAV increase from $23.23 to $28.00, though it later declined in Q1 2025.
author: Pritam Borbora
